architecture_violations.description: Direct access to data layer from presentation layer or violation of separation between concerns

locations.violation: Controllers accessing models directly without service layer
locations.description: Controllers contain business logic that should be in service layer, calling models directly for complex operations
examples: campaignController.launchCampaign does domain selection, recipient processing, job creation - all business logic
locations.suggested_refactor: Introduce service layer between controllers and models to encapsulate business logic

locations.description: The /validate-emails endpoint contains validation logic that should be in service/util layer
examples: Email validation and MX checking logic in route handler
locations.suggested_refactor: Move validation logic to emailVerifier service and call from route

locations.description: Server contains database repair logic and event processing that should be in services
examples: Postfix log parser event handling with direct model access
locations.suggested_refactor: Move database operations to appropriate services or repositories

architecture_violations.description: Missing clear boundaries between concerns leading to tight coupling

locations.violation: Tight coupling between email sending and warmup/throttling logic
locations.description: Email sending process should not need to know about warmup limits - this should be abstracted
examples: Worker checks warmup limits before sending (inferred from architecture)
WarmupConfig slot reservation is called from somewhere in sending flow
locations.suggested_refactor: Use dependency injection or events to decouple sending from throttling

locations.violation: Tight coupling between Postfix parser and delivery event storage
locations.description: Parser directly saves to DeliveryEvent model instead of using repository or service
locations.suggested_refactor: Introduce delivery event repository or service abstraction

locations.violation: Tight coupling between tracking logic and HTTP layer
locations.description: Tracking routes directly create Tracking model instances instead of using service
locations.suggested_refactor: Introduce tracking service to encapsulate tracking logic

architecture_violations.description: Services that are too broad or too narrow in their responsibilities

locations.violation: RelayPool handles both global relay creation and user-specific transport creation
locations.description: RelayPool mixes concerns of global relay management and user-specific transport creation

locations.violation: PostfixLogParser handles both parsing and persistence
locations.description: Parser mixes log parsing logic with database persistence
examples: _addToBuffer method does both parsing and DeliveryEvent.create()
locations.suggested_refactor: Separate parser (pure parsing) from persistence service

architecture_violations.description: Classes or functions that have more than one reason to change

locations.description: Handles log parsing, event building, buffering, stats tracking, database persistence, and event emission
locations.suggested_refactor: Split into LogParser, EventBuilder, BufferManager, PersistenceService, StatsTracker, and ReconnectionManager

locations.description: Handles authentication, routing, data loading, state management, UI layout, and error handling
examples: Layout structure (header, sidebar, main content, FAB)
locations.suggested_refactor: Split into AuthContext, Layout components, Data service, and route-specific components

locations.description: Handles domain management, verification, DKIM generation, importing, and testing
locations.suggested_refactor: Split into domain service, verification service, import service, and keep controller as thin interface

summary: The codebase shows significant architecture violations, primarily layer violations (business logic in controllers), missing abstraction boundaries (tight coupling between layers), and violations of Single Responsibility Principle (God objects like PostfixLogParser, App.jsx, and domainController). These issues make the code harder to maintain, test, and extend.
